message:
fix(checkpoint): correct parseGitStatus for unstaged files and update test expectations

- Fix parseGitStatus to use TrimRight instead of TrimSpace to preserve
  leading spaces in git porcelain format (space is significant for
  detecting staged vs unstaged files)
- Update TestSanitizeName to expect 50-char limit (was incorrectly 48)
- Update TestGenerateID to expect 19/26 chars (includes milliseconds)
- Fix TestTemplate_Execute to provide required 'file' variable

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-pp5.1.1","title":"Create internal/history package with JSONL storage","description":"Implement the history storage layer using JSONL format.

## Package Structure
```
internal/history/
├── entry.go      # HistoryEntry struct
├── storage.go    # File I/O operations
└── query.go      # Search/filter functions
```

## HistoryEntry Type
```go
// internal/history/entry.go
package history

import "time"

type HistoryEntry struct {
    ID        string    `json:"id"`
    Timestamp time.Time `json:"ts"`
    Session   string    `json:"session"`
    Targets   []string  `json:"targets"`
    Prompt    string    `json:"prompt"`
    Source    string    `json:"source"`    // cli, palette, replay
    Template  string    `json:"template,omitempty"`
    Success   bool      `json:"success"`
    Error     string    `json:"error,omitempty"`
}
```

## Storage Functions
```go
// internal/history/storage.go
package history

import (
    "encoding/json"
    "os"
    "path/filepath"
)

const historyFile = "history.jsonl"

// StoragePath returns ~/.local/share/ntm/history.jsonl
func StoragePath() string {
    dataDir := os.Getenv("XDG_DATA_HOME")
    if dataDir == "" {
        home, _ := os.UserHomeDir()
        dataDir = filepath.Join(home, ".local", "share")
    }
    return filepath.Join(dataDir, "ntm", historyFile)
}

// Append adds an entry to history
func Append(entry HistoryEntry) error {
    path := StoragePath()
    
    // Ensure directory exists
    if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0755); err \!= nil {
        return err
    }
    
    f, err := os.OpenFile(path, os.O_APPEND|os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0644)
    if err \!= nil {
        return err
    }
    defer f.Close()
    
    data, err := json.Marshal(entry)
    if err \!= nil {
        return err
    }
    
    _, err = f.Write(append(data, '\n'))
    return err
}

// ReadAll reads all history entries
func ReadAll() ([]HistoryEntry, error) {
    // Read file line by line, parse JSON
}

// ReadRecent reads last N entries (efficient: read from end)
func ReadRecent(n int) ([]HistoryEntry, error) {
    // Use tail-like reading for efficiency
}
```

## ID Generation
Use ULID for sortable, unique IDs:
```go
import "github.com/oklog/ulid/v2"

func NewID() string {
    return ulid.Make().String()
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Append is atomic (no partial writes)
- [ ] ReadRecent is efficient (doesn't read entire file)
- [ ] Handles missing file gracefully
- [ ] Creates directory if needed
- [ ] JSON is valid per line","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:47:12.096526-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T04:20:15.543518-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T04:20:15.543518-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-pp5.1.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-pp5.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:47:12.09686-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-pp5.1.2","title":"Integrate history logging into ntm send command","description":"Modify the send command to log all prompts to history.

## Current State
internal/cli/send.go sends prompts but doesn't log them.

## Changes Required

### Import history package
```go
import "github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/history"
```

### Log after sending
```go
func runSend(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    // ... existing setup ...
    
    // Prepare history entry
    entry := history.HistoryEntry{
        ID:        history.NewID(),
        Timestamp: time.Now(),
        Session:   sessionName,
        Prompt:    prompt,
        Source:    "cli",
    }
    
    // Collect targets as we send
    var targets []string
    var sendErr error
    
    for _, pane := range selectedPanes {
        targets = append(targets, pane.Title)
        if err := sendToPane(pane, prompt); err \!= nil {
            sendErr = err
            // Don't break - continue to other panes
        }
    }
    
    // Complete entry
    entry.Targets = targets
    entry.Success = sendErr == nil
    if sendErr \!= nil {
        entry.Error = sendErr.Error()
    }
    
    // Log to history (ignore errors - don't fail send for history)
    if err := history.Append(entry); err \!= nil {
        // Log warning but don't fail
        fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to log to history: %v\n", err)
    }
    
    return sendErr
}
```

### Add --no-history flag
```go
cmd.Flags().Bool("no-history", false, "Don't log this prompt to history")
```

### Template Integration
If using --template, record template name:
```go
if templateName \!= "" {
    entry.Template = templateName
}
```

## Also Update Palette Send
internal/palette/model.go or wherever palette sends prompts:
- Same pattern: create entry, send, log
- Source = "palette"

## Testing
- Verify history.jsonl is created on first send
- Verify entry format matches schema
- Verify --no-history suppresses logging
- Verify errors are recorded but send continues

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every ntm send logs to history
- [ ] Palette sends also log to history  
- [ ] --no-history flag works
- [ ] Failed sends are logged with error
- [ ] Template name recorded when used","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:47:34.225928-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T04:27:36.819182-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T04:27:36.819182-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-pp5.1.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-pp5.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:47:34.227579-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-pp5.1.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-pp5.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:47:34.228462-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-pp5.2","title":"Prompt Replay Command","description":"New `ntm replay` command to re-send historical prompts.

## Command Interface
```bash
ntm replay 5                     # Re-send prompt #5 (from history)
ntm replay 01HXYZ...             # Re-send by ID
ntm replay --last                # Re-send most recent prompt
ntm replay 5 --to=cod            # Send to different targets
ntm replay 5 --session=other     # Send to different session
ntm replay 5 --edit              # Edit before sending
ntm replay 5 --dry-run           # Show what would be sent
```

## Lookup Behavior
1. If arg is a number, treat as index (1 = most recent)
2. If arg is a string, treat as ID prefix (match start)
3. `--last` is sugar for index 1

## Example Flow
```
$ ntm history
 #  TIME        SESSION      TARGETS         PROMPT (truncated)
 1  10:31:00    myproject    cc_1,cc_2       implement JWT authentication...
 2  10:30:00    myproject    cod_1           run all tests and fix failures
 3  10:25:00    otherproject gmi_1           analyze this codebase for...

$ ntm replay 1
Re-sending to myproject (cc_1, cc_2):
  "implement JWT authentication..."
Sent to 2 agents.

$ ntm replay 2 --to=cc --session=myproject
Re-sending to myproject (cc_1, cc_2):
  "run all tests and fix failures"
Sent to 2 agents.
```

## Edit Mode
When --edit is specified:
1. Write prompt to temp file
2. Open in 
3. Read modified content
4. Confirm and send

```go
func editPrompt(original string) (string, error) {
    f, _ := os.CreateTemp("", "ntm-prompt-*.md")
    f.WriteString(original)
    f.Close()
    
    editor := os.Getenv("EDITOR")
    if editor == "" { editor = "vim" }
    
    cmd := exec.Command(editor, f.Name())
    cmd.Stdin = os.Stdin
    cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
    cmd.Run()
    
    modified, _ := os.ReadFile(f.Name())
    return string(modified), nil
}
```

## Flags
| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| --last | Replay most recent prompt |
| --to | Override targets (--cc, --cod, --gmi, or pane names) |
| --session | Send to different session |
| --edit | Edit prompt before sending |
| --dry-run | Show what would be sent |
| --no-history | Don't log this replay |

## Files to Create
- internal/cli/replay.go - Command implementation

## Dependencies

-{"id":"ntm-pp5.3","title":"Prompt Templates with Variables","description":"Named templates with variable substitution for reusable prompts.

## Overview
Templates are named, parameterized prompts stored in files.
Users can invoke them via `ntm send --template=name`.

## Storage Locations
1. Global: ~/.config/ntm/templates/*.md
2. Project: .ntm/templates/*.md (if Per-Project Config enabled)

## Template Format
```markdown
---
name: code_review
description: Review code for quality issues
variables:
  file: File path to review
  focus: What to focus on (optional)
---

Review the file {{file}} for:
- Code quality issues
- Potential bugs
- Performance concerns
{{#focus}}
Focus especially on: {{focus}}
{{/focus}}

Be thorough but concise.
```

## Variable Types

### Required Variables
`{{variable}}` - Must be provided via --var

### Optional Variables (with conditionals)
`{{#variable}}...{{/variable}}` - Section only included if variable provided

### Built-in Variables
| Variable | Value |
|----------|-------|
| `{{file}}` | Content of --file argument |
| `{{files}}` | Content of all --file arguments |
| `{{selection}}` | Current editor selection (if available) |
| `{{clipboard}}` | Clipboard content |
| `{{cwd}}` | Current working directory |
| `{{session}}` | Session name |
| `{{date}}` | Current date |
| `{{time}}` | Current time |

## Command Interface
```bash
# Use template
ntm send myproject --template=code_review --var file=src/auth.py

# Use with file injection
ntm send myproject -t code_review --file=src/auth.py
# {{file}} auto-populated with file contents

# List available templates
ntm template list

# Show template content
ntm template show code_review

# Create new template
ntm template create my_template
# Opens editor with scaffold
```

## Template Resolution
1. Look in project templates (.ntm/templates/)
2. Look in global templates (~/.config/ntm/templates/)
3. Look in built-in templates (embedded in binary)

## Built-in Templates
Ship with sensible defaults:
- code_review: Review code for issues
- explain: Explain how code works
- refactor: Refactor code
- test: Write tests
- document: Add documentation

## Parsing Implementation
```go
func ParseTemplate(content string, vars map[string]string) (string, error) {
    // Parse frontmatter
    meta, body := splitFrontmatter(content)
    
    // Check required variables
    for _, v := range meta.Variables {
        if _, ok := vars[v.Name]; \!ok && v.Required {
            return "", fmt.Errorf("missing required variable: %s", v.Name)
        }
    }
    
    // Expand conditionals
    body = expandConditionals(body, vars)
    
    // Substitute variables
    body = substituteVariables(body, vars)
    
    return body, nil
}
```

## Files to Create
- internal/templates/loader.go - Find and load templates
- internal/templates/parser.go - Parse template syntax
- internal/templates/builtin.go - Embedded default templates
- internal/cli/template.go - Template management commands","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"RedCreek","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:41:04.28723-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T10:01:49.472762-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-pp5.3","depends_on_id":"ntm-pp5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:41:04.287592-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

```go
func ParseTemplate(content string, vars map[string]string) (string, error) {

            return "", fmt.Errorf("missing required variable: %s", v.Name)
        }
    }
    
    // Expand conditionals
    body = expandConditionals(body, vars)
    
    // Substitute variables
    body = substituteVariables(body, vars)
    
    return body, nil
}
```

- internal/templates/parser.go - Parse template syntax
- internal/templates/builtin.go - Embedded default templates
- internal/cli/template.go - Template management commands","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"RedCreek","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:41:04.28723-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T10:08:56.501011-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T10:08:56.501011-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-pp5.3","depends_on_id":"ntm-pp5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:41:04.287592-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-pp5.4","title":"History Command (ntm history)","description":"CLI command to view and search prompt history.

## Command Interface
```bash
ntm history                          # Show recent prompts
ntm history --session=myproject      # Filter by session
ntm history --limit=50               # Show last 50
ntm history --since=1h               # Last hour
ntm history --search='auth'          # Search prompt text
ntm history --json                   # Output as JSON
ntm history clear                    # Clear all history
ntm history clear --before=7d        # Clear older than 7 days
```

## Default Output
```
 #  TIME        SESSION      TARGETS     PROMPT
 1  10:31:00    myproject    cc_1,cc_2   implement JWT authentication for the API...
 2  10:30:00    myproject    cod_1       run all tests and fix any failures that...
 3  10:25:00    backend      gmi_1       analyze this codebase for security vulne...
 4  10:20:00    myproject    all (5)     review the changes and suggest improveme...
 5  10:15:00    frontend     cc_1        create a React component for user login...

Showing 5 of 127 entries. Use --limit for more.
```

## Detailed View
```bash
ntm history show 1
```
```
ID:       01HXYZ...
Time:     2025-12-10 10:31:00
Session:  myproject
Targets:  cc_1, cc_2
Source:   cli
Template: (none)

Prompt:
  implement JWT authentication for the API. Include:
  - Token generation
  - Token validation
  - Refresh token flow
  - Middleware for protected routes

Status: Success
```

## Flags
| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| --limit, -n | Number of entries (default: 20) |
| --session, -s | Filter by session name |
| --since | Time filter (1h, 1d, 1w) |
| --search | Search prompt text |
| --source | Filter by source (cli, palette, replay) |
| --json | JSON output |

## Subcommands
- `ntm history` - List (default)
- `ntm history show <id>` - Show details
- `ntm history clear` - Clear history
- `ntm history export` - Export to file

## Implementation
```go
func newHistoryCmd() *cobra.Command {
    cmd := &cobra.Command{
        Use:   "history",
        Short: "View prompt history",
        RunE:  runHistoryList,
    }
    
    cmd.AddCommand(
        &cobra.Command{Use: "show", RunE: runHistoryShow},
        &cobra.Command{Use: "clear", RunE: runHistoryClear},
        &cobra.Command{Use: "export", RunE: runHistoryExport},
    )
    
    return cmd
}
```

 {"id":"ntm-yyh","title":"Integration Test: status detection with real panes","description":"# Integration Test: Status Detection

## Scope
Verify agent status detection works with real tmux pane output.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Idle Detection
- Create pane with shell prompt
- Run status detection
- Verify detected as idle

### Scenario 2: Working Detection
- Create pane with active output
- Run status detection
- Verify detected as working

### Scenario 3: Error Detection
- Create pane with error message (rate limit, auth, etc.)
- Run status detection
- Verify error detected and classified

### Scenario 4: Agent Type Detection
- Create panes with different agent titles
- Run type detection
- Verify Claude, Codex, Gemini correctly identified

### Scenario 5: ANSI Handling
- Create pane with colored output
- Run detection
- Verify ANSI codes don't interfere

## Implementation
- tests/integration/status_test.go
- Create real tmux panes
- Send specific content via tmux send-keys
- Run detection functions

 {"id":"ntm-z2k","title":"Update pane naming to include model identifier","description":"# Update Pane Naming to Include Variant Identifier

## Purpose

Enhance pane titles to include model OR persona information for identification and filtering.

## CRITICAL: Resolving Naming Conflict

Both Multi-Model and Personas want to add info to pane names. We need ONE consistent scheme.

**Decision: Use "variant" as a unified concept**
- Variant = persona name (if persona specified)
- Variant = model alias (if only model specified)
- Variant = empty (if neither, for backwards compatibility)

## Naming Format

`{session}__{type}_{index}[_{variant}]`

Examples:
```
myproject__cc_1              # No variant (default model, no persona)
myproject__cc_1_opus         # Model variant (--cc=1:opus)
myproject__cc_1_architect    # Persona variant (--persona=architect)
myproject__cod_2_gpt4        # Model variant
myproject__gmi_1_reviewer    # Persona variant
```

## Priority Rules

1. If --persona specified: Use persona name as variant
2. Else if model specified: Use model alias as variant
3. Else: No variant (backwards compatible)

Rationale: Persona implies model (persona includes model setting), so persona takes precedence.

## Parsing Logic

```go
// Regex: session__type_index[_variant]
var paneNameRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`^(.+)__(\w+)_(\d+)(?:_(\w+))?$`)

type PaneInfo struct {
    Session string
    Type    AgentType  // cc, cod, gmi
    Index   int
    Variant string     // model alias OR persona name, may be empty
}

func ParsePaneName(title string) (*PaneInfo, error) {
    matches := paneNameRegex.FindStringSubmatch(title)
    if matches == nil {
        return nil, ErrInvalidPaneName
    }
    return &PaneInfo{
        Session: matches[1],
        Type:    AgentType(matches[2]),
        Index:   parseInt(matches[3]),
        Variant: matches[4], // May be empty string
    }, nil
}
```

## Variant Type Detection

To know if variant is persona or model:
```go
func (p *PaneInfo) VariantType() VariantType {
    if p.Variant == "" {
        return VariantNone
    }
    if cfg.IsPersonaName(p.Variant) {
        return VariantPersona
    }
    return VariantModel
}
```

## Filtering Support

```bash
ntm send myproject --cc:opus "..."      # Filter by model
ntm send myproject --cc:architect "..." # Filter by persona
ntm send myproject --cc "..."           # All Claude agents
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Pane names include variant when persona or model specified
- [ ] Persona takes precedence over model for variant
- [ ] Backwards compatible with existing panes (no variant)
- [ ] ParsePaneName handles all formats
- [ ] Filtering works by variant (model or persona)
- [ ] Dashboard and status show variant info","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteMountain","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:21:43.467199-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T10:01:33.30816-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T10:01:33.30816-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-z2k","depends_on_id":"ntm-057","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:21:53.966599-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

-{"id":"ntm-zh1e","title":"Add scanner configuration system (.ntm.yaml)","description":"## Overview
Create a configuration system for UBS scanner integration, allowing customization of scan behavior, thresholds, and tool selection.

## Background
Different projects have different needs - some want strict scanning, others more lenient. A configuration system enables:
- Per-project customization
- Consistent behavior across team
- Easy toggling of features

## Implementation Details

### Config File Location
Search order:
1. .ntm.yaml in project root
2. ~/.config/ntm/config.yaml (global defaults)
3. Built-in defaults

### Config Schema
```yaml
scanner:
  # UBS executable path (auto-detected if not set)
  ubs_path: ~/projects/ultimate_bug_scanner/ubs
  
  # Default scan settings
  defaults:
    timeout: 60s
    parallel: true
    exclude:
      - vendor/**
      - node_modules/**
      - "*.test.go"
  
  # Severity thresholds for different contexts
  thresholds:
    pre_commit:
      block_critical: true
      block_errors: 5
    ci:
      fail_critical: true
      fail_errors: 0
    dashboard:
      show_warnings: true
      show_info: false
  
  # Tool selection (which UBS tools to run)
  tools:
    enabled:
      - semgrep
      - gosec
      - staticcheck
    disabled:
      - bandit  # Python not used in this project
  
  # Auto-bead creation settings
  beads:
    auto_create: true
    min_severity: error
    auto_close: true
    
  # Notification settings
  notifications:
    enabled: true
    on_new_critical: true
    summary_after_scan: true
```

### Files to Create/Modify
- internal/config/scanner.go - Scanner config types
- internal/config/loader.go - Config loading logic
- internal/scanner/config.go - Config integration

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Config file parsed correctly
- [ ] Defaults applied when config missing
- [ ] Config validation with helpful errors
- [ ] Environment variable overrides supported","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlackSnow","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:34:11.668449-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T10:05:26.103081-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-zh1e","depends_on_id":"ntm-p77j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:35:03.091094-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-zi5r","title":"EPIC: Session Analytics & Metrics","description":"# Session Analytics & Metrics

## Background & Motivation

Power users running many sessions need insights into their NTM usage patterns.

## CRITICAL: Honest About What We Can Track

**We can ONLY track NTM-initiated actions.** We cannot see:
- Agent-side API calls
- Actual token consumption
- What agents do between our prompts
- Agent "success" or "failure" (subjective)

**What we CAN track:**
- NTM commands executed
- Sessions created/destroyed
- Agents spawned/crashed
- Prompts sent via `ntm send`
- Session durations
- Pane activity (tmux data)

## What This Feature Provides

### 1. NTM Usage Statistics
```bash
ntm analytics
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ NTM Usage (Last 7 days)                                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Sessions created:     42                                    │
│ Avg session duration: 2.3 hours                             │
│ Agents spawned:       168                                   │
│ Prompts sent (ntm send): 347                               │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ By Agent Type:                                              │
│   Claude:  89 spawned │ 203 prompts sent                   │
│   Codex:   52 spawned │ 98 prompts sent                    │
│   Gemini:  27 spawned │ 46 prompts sent                    │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Peak usage: Weekdays 9-11am, 2-4pm                         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 2. Session History
```bash
ntm analytics sessions
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Recent Sessions                                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ api-refactor    │ 4h 12m │ 8 agents │ 42 prompts │ today  │
│ auth-feature    │ 2h 45m │ 6 agents │ 23 prompts │ today  │
│ test-coverage   │ 1h 30m │ 4 agents │ 15 prompts │ yday   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 3. Context Estimate Trends (Optional)
If Context Window Intelligence is enabled, show trends:
```bash
ntm analytics context
# Average context usage at session end: ~65%
# Sessions ended due to high context: 3
```

## What We're NOT Tracking

- ❌ Actual API token usage (we don't have access)
- ❌ Agent "success rate" (undefined metric)
- ❌ Code quality metrics (subjective)
- ❌ Prompt content (privacy, unless opted in)

## Storage

~/.config/ntm/analytics/events.jsonl
```json
{"ts":"2025-12-10T10:00:00Z","event":"session_create","session":"myproject"}
{"ts":"2025-12-10T10:01:00Z","event":"agent_spawn","session":"myproject","type":"cc","model":"opus"}
{"ts":"2025-12-10T10:05:00Z","event":"prompt_send","session":"myproject","target":"cc","chars":150}
```

30-day retention, automatic rotation.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Event logging for NTM actions
- [ ] ntm analytics shows usage summary
- [ ] Per-agent-type breakdowns
- [ ] Session history with duration
- [ ] Export to JSON/CSV
- [ ] Clear about what's tracked vs not
- [ ] 30-day retention policy","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:27:34.878202-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:36:04.340541-05:00"}
